medical_history.history_of_present_illness: prominent myoclonus and cerebellar ataxia, generalized myoclonus, action induced, and stimulus-sensitive, cerebellar signs included nystagmus, dysmetria, and tremor, profound visual loss, rod cone dysfunction, exotropia, difficulty initiating saccades, mild spasticity, and axonal sensory-motor neuropathy leading to trophic changes, developed scoliosis, wheelchair-bound and dependent for all the activities of daily living
medical_history.family_history: consanguineous parents, 20-year-old brother similarly though more mildly affected

physical_examination.specialty_examination: gaze-evoked nystagmus, difficulties initiating saccades, generalized myoclonus, stimulus-sensitive (tactile, acoustic), cerebellar ataxia, spasticity, distal reflex loss

radiographic_examinations.findings: Cerebellar atrophy, T2/FLAIR hyperintensities, and cavitations in the cerebellum

other_tests.findings: Homozygous mutation in the mitochondrial gene SLC25A46 (c.413T>G;p.L138R)
Five candidate homozygous mutations survived the filtering strategy, with SLC25A46 identified as the top candidate
